title: Magazines

vocabulary.meaning: a piece of writing in a magazine or newspaper
a person who prepares writing for publication
the front page of a magazine
to produce something for people to read
smooth, shiny, and attractive in appearance

questions: What comes to mind when you hear the word 'magazines'?
What were the first magazines you started reading?
Do you subscribe to any magazines?
What do you think of the selection of magazines usually found in dental clinics, doctor's surgeries and hair salons?
What's your favourite magazine?
Do you think there are magazines for every kind of hobby and interest?
Do you read magazines that are published especially for English learners?
Do you think magazines in English are better than those in your own language?
Do you keep magazines after you've read them or do you throw them away?
Do you ever read online magazines? Do you think they are better than paper magazines?
Do you read magazines in English?
What kinds of magazines are there in your country?
Which do you like better, magazines or newspapers?
Do you ever read or look at magazine's written for members of the opposite sex - men's magazine and women's magazines?
How different do you think magazines are in Arab countries, Japan and Sweden?
What kinds of magazines in your country are on the top shelf? Is this where they belong?
Are magazines the best thing to use to kill insects in your house?
Would you prefer to read a fashion, gossip, sports or car magazine?
What magazine would you like to see created that would be perfect for you?
If your face could be on the cover of any magazine in the world, what publication would you choose, and why?
